evals.prompt:
Write a formal academic research report from these survived findings. Topic: 'energy efficiency of large language model inference'. Include all required sections.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_energy_1 | title: Flash attention 2 reduces GPU memory bandwidth consumption by 37% on A100 hardware | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.08691 | author: Dao | year: 2023
- id: urn:mif:f_energy_2 | title: Mixture-of-experts routing reduces average activated parameters to 12-15% of total model size | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04088 | author: Jiang et al. | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_energy_3 | title: Transformer inference energy scales approximately quadratically with sequence length at current hardware utilization rates | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/scaling-energy-2026 | author: Chen et al. | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: A formal academic research report with all six required sections in order: Abstract (150-250 words), Background/Related Context, Method, Findings, Discussion, References. Author-date citations in text (e.g., '(Dao, 2023)') resolving to a full References section. No raw urn:mif: identifiers in prose. Hedged, qualified claims at academic altitude. Full reference entries for all three sources.

deterministic_checks.description: Author-date citation format used in prose (e.g., '(Dao, 2023)')
At least one citation URL from the MIF findings appears in the References section

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose or body text
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_energy_' must not appear as visible body text in the report. Internal MIF IDs are resolved to author-date citations or [N] footnotes — never exposed to the reader.

expectations.description: The Abstract covers question, method, principal findings, and conclusion within approximately 150-250 words
expectations.criteria: The Abstract section states: (1) the research question or topic, (2) how findings were gathered or validated, (3) the principal findings (e.g., flash attention, MoE routing, quadratic scaling), and (4) a conclusion. It should be self-contained and approximately 150-250 words.

expectations.description: The Method section explains how findings were verified, including how the falsification gate was applied
expectations.criteria: The Method section states that findings were adversarially verified (e.g., falsification gate) and describes what happened to findings with different verdicts. It does not skip or gloss over the verification step.

expectations.description: The Discussion section includes limitations or threats to validity, not just a restatement of findings
expectations.criteria: The Discussion section explicitly names at least one limitation, constraint, threat to validity, or open question — e.g., scope limitations, evidence quality, hardware generalizability, or missing dimensions. It does not merely restate findings from the Findings section.

evals.prompt:
Produce a formal academic research report on 'agentic AI system failure modes'. The finding set includes one falsified finding and one weakened finding — handle each according to the academic genre rules.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_agent_1 | title: Prompt injection attacks succeed against 67% of tested autonomous agents in red-team evaluations by Perez and Ribeiro | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/prompt-injection-study | author: Perez and Ribeiro | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_agent_2 | title: Agentic systems exhibit goal drift in 100% of extended autonomous runs exceeding 48 hours with no human checkpoint | dimension: behavioral | verdict: falsified | citation: https://example.com/goal-drift-claim | author: Huang et al. | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_agent_3 | title: Tool-call error rates increase by approximately 3x when context window utilization exceeds 80% of capacity | dimension: technical | verdict: weakened | citation: https://example.com/context-degradation-study | author: Kim et al. | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: Academic report that: (1) excludes the falsified finding (f_agent_2 / 100% goal drift claim — must not appear in Findings section), (2) includes the weakened finding (f_agent_3) but annotates it as weakened/uncertain, (3) includes the survived finding (f_agent_1) as settled evidence, (4) maintains all six required sections, (5) uses author-date citations.

deterministic_checks.description: Survived finding f_agent_1 (Perez and Ribeiro prompt injection study) appears in the report
Weakened finding f_agent_3 (Kim et al. context degradation) appears in the report (weakened = include with annotation, not exclude)
Author-date citation format present for at least one survived or weakened finding

expectations.description: The falsified finding (Huang et al., 100% goal drift in 100% of runs) does NOT appear in the Findings section as evidence
expectations.criteria: The claim that agentic systems exhibit goal drift in 100% of runs must not appear as a supported finding. The Huang et al. 2025 study and the '100%' goal-drift figure must be absent from the Findings section. The falsified finding may only be mentioned in Method (describing exclusion policy) — never as evidence.

expectations.description: The weakened finding (Kim et al., 3x tool-call error rate) is presented with an explicit uncertainty qualifier, not as settled fact
expectations.criteria: The Kim et al. finding about tool-call error rates must carry an inline qualifier such as 'weakened evidence suggests', 'preliminary findings indicate', 'contested', 'weakened verdict', or similar. It must not be stated with the same confidence as the survived Perez/Ribeiro finding.

expectations.description: The Method section explicitly states that falsified findings are excluded and that weakened findings are annotated rather than dropped
expectations.criteria: The Method section describes the verification policy: falsified findings are excluded from evidence; weakened/inconclusive findings are retained with explicit annotation. This must be stated as methodology, not implied.

evals.prompt:
Write an academic research report from these findings. Topic: 'retrieval-augmented generation accuracy benchmarks'. All four findings survived falsification. Citations are mandatory — every claim must trace to a finding.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_rag_1 | title: RAGAS framework achieves 0.89 faithfulness score on HotpotQA when top-3 chunks are retrieved | dimension: evaluation | verdict: survived | citation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.15217 | author: Es et al. | year: 2023
- id: urn:mif:f_rag_2 | title: Hybrid sparse-dense retrieval outperforms dense-only retrieval by 7-12 percentage points on multi-hop questions | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/hybrid-retrieval-2025 | author: Zhao and Park | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_rag_3 | title: Chunk size of 256 tokens outperforms 512-token chunks on precision for fact-dense technical documents | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/chunking-ablation | author: Nakamura et al. | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_rag_4 | title: Re-ranking with a cross-encoder adds 4-6% absolute NDCG improvement over bi-encoder retrieval alone | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/reranking-study | author: Fernandez | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: A complete academic research report with all six sections. Every factual claim in Findings traces to one of the four supplied findings with an author-date citation. No uncited assertions. No urn:mif: leakage in prose. References section lists all four sources with URLs.

deterministic_checks.description: Finding f_rag_1 (RAGAS faithfulness score) referenced in the report
Finding f_rag_2 (hybrid retrieval) referenced in the report
Finding f_rag_3 (chunk size ablation) referenced in the report
Finding f_rag_4 (re-ranking NDCG improvement) referenced in the report
At least one author-date inline citation (Es et al. 2023, Zhao and Park 2025, Nakamura et al. 2024, or Fernandez 2026) appears in prose

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or f_rag_N internal IDs appear in the body text or Findings section
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:', 'f_rag_1', 'f_rag_2', 'f_rag_3', 'f_rag_4' must not appear as visible prose in the report body. All references are author-date citations (e.g., '(Es et al., 2023)') or numbered footnotes.

expectations.description: The References section lists all four sources with their URLs and is alphabetically ordered
expectations.criteria: The References section contains entries for Es et al. 2023 (arxiv.org/abs/2309.15217), Zhao and Park 2025, Nakamura et al. 2024, and Fernandez 2026, each with the citation URL from the supplied finding. The list is alphabetical by first author surname.

expectations.description: The Discussion section names at least one genuine limitation rather than merely restating findings
expectations.criteria: The Discussion section includes an honest limitation: e.g., benchmark generalizability, dataset scope (HotpotQA is a single benchmark), hardware or corpus dependencies of chunk-size findings, or open questions about real-world vs. benchmark performance. It does not merely recount what the Findings section established.
